name: Ḥájí Mírzá Ḥasan Ḵhurásání
summary: A Bábí listed among the captives bound in chains in The Dawn-Breakers.
importance_reason: One-mention incidental name in a captive list, no developed story.

name: Ḥájí Mírzá Ḥasan-i-Ḥakím-Báshí
summary: A physician (ḥakím-báshí) in Baghdád in whose house Bahá'u'lláh was a guest, where Mírzá Áqá Ján first met Him.
importance_reason: Single passing mention; notable only as host at a moment of Mírzá Áqá Ján's conversion.

name: Ḥájí Mírzá Ḥasan-i-Ḵhurásání
summary: A Bahá'í teacher whom 'Abdu'l-Bahá dispatched as a messenger to dispel doubts in the community.
importance_reason: One-mention messenger of 'Abdu'l-Bahá; minor teaching role.

name: Ḥájí Mírzá Ḥasan-i-Ṣafá
summary: An accomplice of Mírzá Ḥusayn Khán who circulated false reports against Bahá'u'lláh; received a memorable final message from Bahá'u'lláh for the Persian Ambassador.
importance_reason: Recurring secondary antagonist with a memorable interview episode, but only two mentions.

name: Ḥájí Mírzá Ḥaydar-‘Alíy-i-Iṣfahání
summary: A renowned and beloved Bahá'í teacher and author exiled nine years to Khartoum; affectionately known as the 'Angel of Carmel' for his later years near 'Abdu'l-Bahá.
importance_reason: Famous teacher and memoirist whose story (exile, devotion) is widely taught despite single GPB mention.
characterizations: GPB: arrested Ḥájí Mírzá Ḥaydar-‘Alí and six of his fellow-believers, and instigated their condemnation to a nine year exile in Khartúm

name: Ḥájí Mírzá ‘Abbás-i-Íravání, Ḥájí Mírzá Áqásí
summary: Grand Vizier of Muḥammad Sháh (1835–1848) and his former tutor; the scheming chief minister who exiled the Báb to Mákú and Chihríq, designated by Shoghi Effendi as the Anti-Christ of the Bábí Dispensation.
importance_reason: 94 mentions, high rel_degree; the dominant Qájár official of the Báb's early ministry and a foremost antagonist.
characterizations: GPB: the idolized tutor of Muḥammad Sháh, a vulgar, false-hearted and fickle-minded schemer
Wikipedia: served as grand vizier of Mohammad Shah Qajar from 1835 to 1848
bahai-library.com: Shoghi Effendi designated Áqásí the Anti-Christ of the Bábí Dispensation

name: Ḥájí Mírzáy-i-Ḥalabí-Sáz
summary: A Bahá'í tinsmith (ḥalabí-sáz) brutally flogged and tortured in an early-20th-century persecution, along with his wife and young son who was killed.
importance_reason: Single mention but a vivid, memorable martyrdom anecdote of a whole family.

name: Ḥájí Naṣíru’d-Dín-i-Qazvíní
summary: A Bábí defender at Shaykh Ṭabarsí who reserved a cow's milk daily to make a pudding for Quddús's table.
importance_reason: One-mention fort participant with a small memorable domestic detail.

name: Ḥájí Nád-‘Alí
summary: A man of Qazvín whose caravanserai in Ṭihrán is named as a meeting point in The Dawn-Breakers.
importance_reason: One-mention incidental; named chiefly for his caravanserai.

name: Ḥájí Náṣir-i-Qazvíní
summary: A Bábí of recognized standing, sent to Ṭihrán among the captives alongside the father of Badí‘.
importance_reason: Two mentions as a notable captive but no developed story.

name: Ḥájí Níyáz-i-Bag̱hdádí
summary: A man of Baghdád who informed the Sherif of Mecca of the Báb's mission and martyrdom.
importance_reason: Three mentions; minor courier role spreading word of the Báb.

name: Ḥájí Qásim-i-Nayrízí
summary: A Bábí comrade of Ḥájí Sulaymán Khán, martyred in Ṭihrán in 1852 by having lighted candles thrust into his flesh and being paraded before the crowd.
importance_reason: Four mentions; martyred with the famous candle-torture, taught alongside Sulaymán Khán.

name: Ḥájí Siyyid Asadu’lláh (son of Ḥájí Siyyid Muḥammad-Báqir)
aliases: Ḥájí Siyyid Asadu’lláh (son of Ḥájí Siyyid Muḥammad-Báqir)
summary: Son and successor of the great mujtahid Ḥájí Siyyid Muḥammad-Báqir-i-Rashtí of Iṣfahán; declined the Mu‘tamid's invitation and acknowledged Mullá Ḥusayn's eloquence over his father.
importance_reason: Six mentions in the Iṣfahán episode; a clerical figure with a recurring but secondary role.

name: Ḥájí Siyyid Báqir-i-Rashtí
summary: A late eminent cleric of Iṣfahán regarded as the 'Proof of Islám' and most learned of doctors.
importance_reason: Single mention; likely a variant reference to Muḥammad-Báqir-i-Rashtí (possible duplicate).

name: Ḥájí Siyyid Ismá‘íl, the S̱hayḵhu’l-Islám of Bavánát
aliases: Ḥájí Siyyid Ismá‘íl, the S̱hayḵhu’l-Islám of Bavánát
summary: A well-known Shaykhu'l-Islám of Bavánát and Bábí of exceptional courage who took up the sword in the Nayríz upheaval.
importance_reason: Three mentions; a brave named episode participant in Nayríz.

name: Ḥájí Siyyid Javád-i-Karbilá'í
summary: A revered Shaykhí divine who knew the Báb from childhood, recognized His station, and remained a steadfast believer through the Bábí and Bahá'í periods.
importance_reason: 13 mentions across both books; a venerable early believer whose lifelong testimony is recounted.

name: Ḥájí Siyyid Muḥammad-Báqir (mujtahid of Iṣfahán)
summary: The eminent mujtahid of Iṣfahán to whom Mullá Ḥusayn once came as Siyyid Káẓim's messenger; dead by the time of later events and succeeded by his son.
importance_reason: Two mentions; almost certainly the same person as Ḥájí Siyyid Muḥammad-Báqir-i-Rashtí (likely duplicate).

name: Ḥájí Siyyid Muḥammad-Báqir-i-Rashtí
summary: The renowned mujtahid of Iṣfahán whose authority extended far beyond the city; a staunch supporter of Siyyid Káẓim until his death before 1260 A.H.
importance_reason: Seven mentions; a leading cleric of the Shaykhí milieu, though not himself a believer.

name: Ḥájí Siyyid Taqí
summary: One of the leading ‘ulamá of Qazvín named in The Dawn-Breakers.
importance_reason: One-mention name in a list of Qazvín clerics.

name: Ḥájí Siyyid ‘Ábid (the treacherous messenger)
summary: A messenger who treacherously betrayed directions he was given to Zaynu'l-‘Ábidín in The Dawn-Breakers.
importance_reason: Three mentions; a minor treacherous figure in a single episode.

name: Ḥájí Sulaymán Khán-i-Tabrízí
summary: An influential and courageous Bábí of Tabríz who secretly recovered and hid the Báb's remains, and was himself martyred in Ṭihrán in 1852 with lighted candles thrust into his flesh, walking to his death in a blaze of fortitude.
importance_reason: 23 mentions; one of the most celebrated and beloved martyrs of the Faith, his death a foremost teaching story.
characterizations: GPB: the high-minded, the influential and courageous Ḥájí Sulaymán Khán ... Standing erect as an arrow, his eyes glowing with stoic fortitude
bahaipedia.org: nine candles were thrust into deep holes cut in his flesh

name: Ḥájí S̱hayḵh Muḥammad
summary: A believer surnamed Nabíl ('noble/learned') by the Báb.
importance_reason: Single mention; notable only for the title bestowed by the Báb.

name: Ḥájí S̱hayḵh ‘Abdu’l-‘Alí
summary: A Bábí chief and father-in-law of Siyyid Yaḥyá (Vaḥíd).
importance_reason: Three mentions; minor figure notable for kinship to Vaḥíd.

name: Ḥájí S̱háh Muḥammad-i-Mans̱hádí, surnamed Amínu’l-Bayán
summary: A trusted Bahá'í, surnamed Amínu'l-Bayán, who received the Báb's remains as a sacred Trust and helped secretly bury the casket beneath the shrine of Imám-Zádih Zayd.
importance_reason: Two mentions; entrusted custodian in the celebrated saga of concealing the Báb's remains.

name: Ḥájí Yaḥyá Khán of Tabríz
summary: The late father of the martyr Ḥájí Sulaymán Khán of Tabríz.
importance_reason: One-mention; identified only as Sulaymán Khán's father.

name: Ḥájí Zaynu’l-‘Ábidín (of Kinár-Gird)
summary: A Bábí whom the Báb bade remain behind to convey His greetings to friends and dissuade them from following Him.
importance_reason: Single mention with a touching first-person quote, but no developed role.

name: Ḥájí Ḥasan-i-Míyámay’í
summary: A Bábí martyr from the village of Míyámay.
importance_reason: One-mention martyr-list entry.

name: Ḥájí Ḥasan-i-Náyíní
summary: An intimate friend and confidant who was entrusted with a secret by his master in The Dawn-Breakers.
importance_reason: Two mentions; minor confidant role.

name: Ḥájí Ḥusayn-i-Kás̱hí
summary: A henchman who espoused the cause of the Covenant-breaker Mírzá Muḥammad-‘Alí.
importance_reason: Single mention; minor Covenant-breaker partisan.

name: Ḥájí Ḥusayn-‘Alí
summary: An esteemed elder uncle of a narrator in Qazvín who was preparing a feast in his honor.
importance_reason: One-mention incidental family member.

name: Ḥájí ‘Abbás-i-Zanjání
summary: A Bábí of Zanján named in The Dawn-Breakers.
importance_reason: One-mention incidental name.

name: Ḥájí ‘Abdu’l-Karím-i-Ṭihrání
summary: A Bahá'í teacher who made conversions and was sent by 'Abdu'l-Bahá among the messengers who dispelled doubts in the community.
importance_reason: Two mentions; minor teacher and messenger of 'Abdu'l-Bahá.

name: Ḥájí ‘Abdu’l-Majíd (father of ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb)
summary: Father of the martyr ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb and a courtier of the Farmán-Farmá in Fárs, who tearfully recounted his son's story.
importance_reason: Two mentions; remembered mainly as a grieving father and narrator.

name: Ḥájí ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb (disciple of S̱hayḵh Aḥmad in Yazd)
aliases: Ḥájí ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb (disciple of S̱hayḵh Aḥmad in Yazd)
summary: A pious, upright and unlettered man of Yazd, awakened to the Cause, who was shown marked preference despite his modesty.
importance_reason: Single mention; a humble early believer with a small anecdote.

name: Ḥájí ‘Alí
summary: The Grand Vizier's trusted emissary sent to the Síyáh-Chál to deliver Bahá'u'lláh's release order, who was so moved by the conditions he beheld that he cursed his master.
importance_reason: Four mentions; minor official with a memorable, sympathetic episode at the Síyáh-Chál.

name: Ḥájí ‘Alí-‘Askar
summary: A narrator who related, via a Russian-consulate relative, the account of a sketch made of the Báb.
importance_reason: One-mention incidental narrator.

name: Ḵhadíjih (wife of Ḥujjat)
summary: Wife of Ḥujjat at the Zanján upheaval; warned of impending captivity, she was killed instantly by a cannon-ball while holding their baby Hádí.
importance_reason: Single mention but a poignant, memorable death at the Zanján siege.

name: Ḵhadíjih-Sulṭán
summary: A sixty-five-year-old Bahá'í woman of Manshád hurled to her death from a rooftop during persecution.
importance_reason: Single-mention martyr with a brief but vivid detail.

name: Ḵhalíl Khán of Savád-Kúh
summary: A figure of Savád-Kúh named with the men of Qádí-Kalá in The Dawn-Breakers.
importance_reason: One-mention name; likely the same as Ḵhalíl Ḵhán-i-Savád-Kúhí (possible duplicate).

name: Ḵhalíl Ḵhán-i-Savád-Kúhí
summary: A man of Savád-Kúh named in The Dawn-Breakers.
importance_reason: One-mention name; almost certainly the same person as 'Ḵhalíl Khán of Savád-Kúh' (likely duplicate).

name: Ḵhalíl-i-Mázindarání
summary: A Bábí martyr of Mázindarán.
importance_reason: One-mention martyr-list entry.

name: Ḵhalíl-i-Ḵhu’í
summary: A henchman who, with others, espoused the cause of the Covenant-breaker Mírzá Muḥammad-‘Alí.
importance_reason: Single mention; minor Covenant-breaker partisan.

name: Ḵhudá-Murád Ḵhán-i-Kurd
summary: A Kurdish figure named in The Dawn-Breakers.
importance_reason: One-mention bare name.

name: Ḵhudádád
summary: A Bábí named among those deemed indispensable to the defense at Shaykh Ṭabarsí.
importance_reason: One-mention fort participant in a list.

name: Ḵhusraw-i-Qádí-Kalá’í
summary: A horseman ordered to escort the Bábís through Shír-Gáh who treacherously led them through the forest to ambush them; later slain.
importance_reason: 14 mentions; a recurring named treacherous figure in the Ṭabarsí narrative.

name: Ḵhán-Muḥammad-i-Túb-C̱hí
summary: A friend of Ḥujjat (an artilleryman, túb-chí) who petitioned to rescue his Master from the enemy.
importance_reason: Single mention; minor Zanján-era figure.

name: Ḵhánlar Mírzá
summary: A Qájár prince who, hating his brother Íldirím Mírzá, had him stripped, chained, and imprisoned at Ardibíl where he died.
importance_reason: Two mentions; minor royal with a memorable act of fratricidal cruelty.

name: Ḵhátún-Ján
summary: Wife of Muḥammad-Hádí, instructed to disguise herself as a beggar to reach the house where Ṭáhirih was confined.
importance_reason: Single mention; minor figure in the Ṭáhirih episode.

name: Ṣadru’d-Dawlih
summary: Grandson of Ḥájí Muḥammad-Ḥusayn Khán of Iṣfahán, who entered the city at the head of the Khamsih tribe.
importance_reason: One-mention; likely distinct from the Zanján-era Ṣadru'd-Dawliy-i-Iṣfahání but title overlaps (note).

name: Ṣadru’d-Dawliy-i-Iṣfahání
summary: A Qájár commander whose ferocious onslaught against the Bábís at the Zanján upheaval failed, leading to his reprimand by the Sháh and degradation from rank.
importance_reason: Six mentions, high rel_degree; a named military antagonist in the Zanján upheaval.

name: Ṣafar
summary: A man whose brother Sha‘bán had perished in the war, who seized Siyyid Yaḥyá (Vaḥíd) by his belt and dragged him on the ground.
importance_reason: Two mentions; a named participant in Vaḥíd's martyrdom.

name: Ṣafar-‘Alí (martyr of Sang-Sar, son of Karbilá’í ‘Alí)
aliases: Ṣafar-‘Alí (martyr of Sang-Sar, son of Karbilá’í ‘Alí)
summary: A Bábí martyr of Sang-Sar, son of Karbilá'í ‘Alí, who labored to awaken the people of Sang-Sar.
importance_reason: One-mention Sang-Sar martyr.

name: Ṣafí-Qulí-i-Mázindarání
summary: A Bábí martyr of Mázindarán.
importance_reason: One-mention martyr-list entry.

name: Ṣaláḥu’d-Dín Yúsuf ibn Ayyúb
summary: The 12th-century Ayyubid sultan Saladin, referenced in GPB for his association with the famed Sunní seat of learning (the Salimiyyah) and its endowments.
importance_reason: Single GPB mention; world-historical figure but only a passing contextual reference in Bahá'í history.
characterizations: GPB: its association with Ṣaláḥu'd-Dín-i-Ayyúbí and his descendants; from it some of the most illustrious exponents of Sunní Islám had gone forth

name: Ṣubḥí Pás̱há
summary: An Ottoman official at ‘Akká who high-handedly summoned Bahá'u'lláh to the government house and subsequently lost his position and was recalled in disgrace.
importance_reason: Two mentions; a named official whose fall illustrates the GPB theme of persecutors' downfall.

name: Ṣádiq-i-Tabrízí
summary: A half-demented young confectioner's assistant of Tabríz who, in despair over the Báb's martyrdom, joined the 1852 attempt on the life of Náṣiri'd-Dín Sháh and was instantly slain — the irresponsible act that triggered the great Bábí persecution.
importance_reason: 13 mentions; the would-be assassin whose deed set off the 1852 catastrophe, a pivotal (if condemned) episode.
characterizations: GPB: a certain Ṣádiq-i-Tabrízí, an assistant in a confectioner's shop in Ṭihrán ... that half-demented youth; the ill-fated Ṣádiq, who was instantly slain on the scene of his attempted crime

name: Ṣárá (wife of the Báb)
summary: A figure referred to in the Báb's Commentary on the Súrih of Joseph as 'Ṣárá,' a Siyyid to whom He was betrothed before the throne of God.
importance_reason: Single mention; theologically interesting but obscure reference.

name: Ṭahmásb-Mírzá
summary: A prince by whose order a large number of Bábís were slain in Shíráz, their heads paraded to Ábádih.
importance_reason: Single mention; a named persecuting official.

name: Ṭáhir-i-Mázindarání
summary: A Bábí martyr of Mázindarán.
importance_reason: One-mention martyr-list entry.

name: Ẓáhir
summary: An Arab Bábí who accompanied Mírzá Yaḥyá in disguise to Mosul to rejoin the exiles bound for Constantinople.
importance_reason: Single mention; minor companion of Mírzá Yaḥyá.

name: ‘Abbás Effendi, ‘Abdu’l-Bahá
summary: The eldest son of Bahá'u'lláh and, by His Covenant, His appointed successor, sole authorized Interpreter, and Center of the Covenant (1844–1921); the perfect Exemplar of the Faith who established it in the West.
importance_reason: 287 mentions, highest rel_degree; the central figure after Bahá'u'lláh and Head of the Faith.
characterizations: GPB: the vibrant personality of ‘Abdu’l-Bahá, mysterious in His essence, unique in His station, astoundingly potent in both the charm and strength of His character

name: ‘Abbás Mírzá, the Náyibu’s-Salṭanih (crown prince, father of Muḥammad Sháh)
aliases: ‘Abbás Mírzá, the Náyibu’s-Salṭanih (crown prince, father of Muḥammad Sháh)
summary: The Qájár crown prince and reformer, father of Muḥammad Sháh, referenced in The Dawn-Breakers as the Náyibu's-Salṭanih.
importance_reason: Single mention; historically significant prince but only a genealogical reference here.

name: ‘Abbás-Qulí Khán (executioner of Vaḥíd, Nayríz)
summary: A man notorious for ruthlessness who volunteered to perform the execution his comrades had sworn not to carry out, in the Nayríz upheaval.
importance_reason: Two mentions; a named perpetrator; distinct from the Láríjání commander of the same forename (note).

name: ‘Abbás-Qulí Khán-i-Láríjání
summary: The Qájár commander at Shaykh Ṭabarsí whose ambush-fired bullet killed Mullá Ḥusayn, the first Letter of the Living; depicted as cowardly, he later boasted of the deed before Prince Aḥmad Mírzá.
importance_reason: 30 mentions, high rel_degree; the slayer of Mullá Ḥusayn, a pivotal antagonist in the Ṭabarsí story.
characterizations: GPB: the cowardly 'Abbás-Qulí Khán-i-Láríjání ... discharged [the bullet], while lying in ambush in the branches of a neighboring tree

name: ‘Abbás-‘Alí-i-Sangsarí
summary: A Bábí martyr of Sang-Sar.
importance_reason: One-mention martyr-list entry.

name: ‘Abdu’l-Ghaffár-i-Iṣfahání
summary: One of four companions condemned to share Mírzá Yaḥyá's exile; in despair he threw himself into the sea crying 'Yá Bahá'u'l-Abhá' and was rescued with difficulty; Bahá'u'lláh praised his detachment and trust in God.
importance_reason: Single mention but a memorable episode and explicit praise from Bahá'u'lláh.

name: ‘Abdu’l-Kháliq-i-Iṣfahání
summary: A learned cleric (an early convert of Mullá Ḥusayn at Nishápúr, surnamed Ḏhabíḥ) who, aghast at the sight of the unveiled Ṭáhirih at Badasht, cut his own throat and fled.
importance_reason: Eight mentions; a learned convert remembered chiefly for the dramatic Badasht episode.

name: ‘Abdu’l-Muḥammad-i-Zanjání
summary: A Bábí of Zanján named in The Dawn-Breakers.
importance_reason: One-mention incidental name.

name: ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb
summary: A youth who tearfully entreated Mullá ‘Alí to let him join his journey and who later suffered martyrdom in Ṭihrán.
importance_reason: Three mentions; a young martyr with a small touching anecdote (one of several distinct ‘Abdu'l-Vahhábs — note).

name: ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb (companion at Núr)
summary: A companion whom Bahá'u'lláh intended to send ahead to announce His approach to the besieged at Ṭabarsí.
importance_reason: Single mention; likely the same as '‘Abdu’l-Vahháb of Núr' (possible duplicate).

name: ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb of Núr
summary: A devoted disciple of the Báb and resident of Núr, shot dead as a martyr alongside Muḥammad-Taqí Khán in the Núr turmoil.
importance_reason: Two mentions; a Núr martyr, possibly the same as '‘Abdu’l-Vahháb (companion at Núr)' (note).

name: ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb-i-Qazvíní (brother of Ṭáhirih)
summary: A learned brother of Ṭáhirih who inherited their father's reputation, was intimidated by her learning, and acknowledged the truth of the Revelation though he did not live up to it.
importance_reason: Two mentions; notable for kinship to Ṭáhirih and his testimony about her.

name: ‘Abdu’l-Ḥamíd Khán
summary: The chief constable of Shíráz who scaled the wall by night to arrest the Báb, then, repentant, threw himself at the Báb's feet and begged forgiveness.
importance_reason: Ten mentions; a named official with a memorable arrest-and-repentance episode.

name: ‘Abdu’l-Ḥamíd-i-Qazvíní
summary: A brother of a narrator who accompanied him to Karbilá and later suffered martyrdom in Ṭihrán.
importance_reason: Single mention; minor figure and martyr.

name: ‘Abdu’l-Ḥaqq-i-Savád-Kúhí
summary: A Bábí martyr of Savád-Kúh.
importance_reason: One-mention martyr-list entry.

name: ‘Abdu’l-Ḥusayn Áyatí (Ávárih)
summary: A once-prominent Bahá'í teacher and historian who apostatized and composed works attacking the Faith and its Administrative Order during the ministry of Shoghi Effendi; his efforts proved abortive.
importance_reason: Single GPB mention but a historically notable apostate ('the shameless apostate of Persia') whose case is taught.
characterizations: GPB: The volumes which a shameless apostate composed and disseminated ... in his brazen efforts not only to disrupt that Order but to undermine the very Faith which had conceived it, proved similarly abortive

name: ‘Abdu’l-Ḥusayn-i-S̱hús̱htarí
summary: A narrator who related an account concerning Mírzá Muḥammad-i-Akhbárí.
importance_reason: One-mention incidental narrator.

name: ‘Abdu’l-‘Alí Ḵhán-i-Marág̱hiyí
summary: Captain of the prince's artillery who professed himself a lover and admirer of Mullá Ḥusayn and volunteered to provision his intended pilgrimage.
importance_reason: Four mentions; a sympathetic officer in the Mullá Ḥusayn narrative.

name: ‘Abdu’l-‘Aẓím-i-Sangsarí
summary: A Bábí martyr of Sang-Sar.
importance_reason: One-mention martyr-list entry.

name: ‘Abdu’lláh Khán, the Shujá‘u’l-Mulk
summary: A Qájár commander, titled Shujá‘u'l-Mulk, who seconded the attack on the Bábís in the first Nayríz upheaval of 1850.
importance_reason: Four mentions, high rel_degree; a named military antagonist in the first Nayríz upheaval.
